title: status_buckets falls back to a hardcoded default status vocabulary (STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS) — DEC-0025 smell (source ships vocabulary).
description: resolveStatusVocabulary (roadmap-plan.ts:111) returns {...STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS, ...(config.status_buckets ?? {})} — a project with no status_buckets inherits a status vocabulary baked into source. Per DEC-0025 (the framework source ships zero vocabulary commitment; vocabulary derives from config/schemas) this is a latent violation, directly parallel to FGAP-056 (PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES hardcodes block kinds). Surfaced while deriving the no-optional / degenerate-default registry semantics: unlike layers (whose degenerate default = 1 implicit layer is legitimate) status_buckets degenerate default is a source-shipped vocabulary, not a structural fallback. Intended outcome: status vocabulary derives from config (or the samples-catalog default), not a source constant; an empty config yields genuine none or a catalog-supplied default, never a source-baked vocabulary.

evidence.reference: resolveStatusVocabulary merges config.status_buckets over the source constant STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS — source ships a status vocabulary
DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral: source ships zero vocabulary) — contradicted by a shipped status vocabulary

impact: A fresh adopter inherits pi-context status vocabulary unbidden; DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutrality is incomplete for status. Same derive-from-registry theme as FGAP-056 (hardcoded block kinds); both are source-shipped-vocabulary smells to resolve via the samples-catalog/config default mechanism.
proposed_resolution: Source the status vocabulary from config / the samples-catalog default rather than a source constant; align with whatever default mechanism the samples catalog establishes. Implementation specifics intentionally NOT prescribed (outcome-only); coordinate with FGAP-056 (same theme) and the samples-catalog default semantics.
